<uploaded_files>/app</uploaded_files>

# Regression QA - RC 2.14: transaction-categorization findings

**Test round:** RC 2.14 pre-release categorization pass
**Prepared by:** QA (enrichment)
**Verdict:** Not signed off - the findings below must clear before this build ships

## Summary

The RC 2.14 pass replays our labelled statement-fixture corpus through the
enrichment stage and diffs every derived field against the accepted baseline.
The overwhelming majority of fixtures reproduce their baseline labels exactly.
A thin residue of rows, though, now derive a category or a counterparty name
that disagrees with the accepted answer. Each regressed row is stable - the same
fixture yields the identical wrong field every time it is replayed - and each one
hinges on a narrow, individually rare pattern in the underlying reference text
rather than on the size of the amount or on which customer it belongs to.
Ordinary purchases, routine transfers and everyday deposits all reproduce
baseline, which is why the aggregate diff read clean until these edge fixtures
were isolated.

The findings are recorded in the order they surfaced. QA has not traced any of
them to a root cause; that is left to engineering. Acceptance for this build is
simply that every listed finding derives its baseline field again and that no
fixture already matching baseline moves.

## Findings

**F-1.** A cheque-type debit whose statement note carries nothing but the
instrument's own number - a short, bare run of digits with no other text sitting
beside it - is landing in the generic transfer bucket. Cheque debits whose notes
are shaped even slightly differently keep their correct cheque label, so the miss
is confined to this one bare-number note form.

**F-2.** On a cleared cheque, the counterparty column is coming back with an
internal clearing tag in place of the account-holder's name. The affected rows
all share one raw-narration layout, copied here from a fixture:

> `CHQ PAID-MICR CLG-RAVI KUMAR`

For this layout the field is returning a fragment of the reference instead of the
name that should be lifted from it.

**F-3.** A slice of inbound electronic-transfer credits - the plain
national-transfer kind - are arriving with the counterparty-name column left
completely blank. The category on these rows is right; it is only the name that
goes missing. Other electronic-transfer credits, whose references are laid out
differently, still resolve their names normally, so this is not a blanket failure
of name extraction.

**F-4.** An incoming person-to-person instant-payment credit is showing a bare
reference number where the sender's name belongs. The value that surfaces is a
fragment of the very reference string the name should have been read from;
neighbouring instant-payment layouts still resolve their names correctly.

**F-5.** A small nominal deposit that a bank posts purely to confirm an account
is reachable - the token "is this account live" credit rather than a real
payment - is being filed as an ordinary transfer instead of being recognised as
an account-verification entry. Ordinary deposits are untouched; only these token
confirmation credits regressed.

## Exit criteria

The enrichment path is fully deterministic and runs offline, so each finding
above is reproducible on demand from the fixture that triggered it. Restore the
correct category and counterparty on the listed forms without disturbing any
fixture that already agrees with baseline. A change that rescues these rows but
shifts the label or the name of ordinary, currently-correct traffic does not meet
acceptance: the bar is that the specific regressions listed here clear and
everything else stays exactly where the baseline has it.

results.content:
<task id="ses_09b9ea823ffec4p3zANpuWKcNL" state="completed">
<task_result>
Here is the comprehensive summary of the codebase at `/app`:

---

## 1. PROJECT TYPE, LANGUAGE, FRAMEWORK, AND BUILD SYSTEM

- **Language:** Python 3.9.2
- **Framework:** Flask (v1.1.2) -- a lightweight WSGI web application framework
- **Build System:** Python setuptools (`setup.py`)
- **Deployment:** Docker + Gunicorn (v20.1.0) -- 4 workers, exposed on port 9898
- **Key Dependencies:** pandas (1.2.4), fuzzywuzzy (0.18.0), python-Levenshtein (0.12.2), scipy (1.7.3), scikit-learn (1.0.1), matplotlib (3.5.1)

---

## 2. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

```
/app/
  .git/
  .gitignore
  Dockerfile
  categorizationapp/
    setup.py
    categorizationapp/
      __init__.py
      Controller.py
      BankScripts/          (35 bank-specific Python modules)
      UtilityScripts/       (14 utility Python modules)
```

There are **no** test directories, no fixture directories, no CSV/JSON/YAML data files, and no separate configuration files anywhere in the repository. All categorization "rules" are defined inline as Python code (regex conditions paired with category labels).

---

## 3. KEY MODULES/PACKAGES

### 3a. Flask Application Entry Point
- **`/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/__init__.py`** -- Creates the Flask `app` object, sets up request timing middleware, imports `Controller.py`.
- **`/app/categorizationapp/setup.py`** -- Package definition (`categorizationapp`), declares all dependencies.

### 3b. Controller / API Router
- **`/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/Controller.py`** (936 lines) -- Defines two Flask routes:
  - `GET /` -- Health check
  - `POST /categorizeTransaction` -- The **main endpoint**. Accepts a JSON payload with `bank`, `acc_type`, `accountUID`, `accountHolder`, `isPFMEnabled`, and `transactions` (array). Dispatches to one of **31 supported bank scripts**, then applies a common enrichment pipeline.
  - `POST /recurrentTransactions` -- Groups transactions by party name similarity using hierarchical clustering.

### 3c. Bank Scripts (35 files)
Each module follows the naming convention `categorize_<BANK>_transactions(transactions, accountType)` and applies bank-specific regex rules to assign a `transactionCategory`. Supported banks:

| File | Bank Identifier |
|---|---|
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Andhra.py` | `ANDHRA_BANK` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/AuSmallFinanceBank.py` | `AU_SMALL_FINANCE_BANK` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Axis.py` | `AXIS` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Bandhan.py` | `BANDHAN` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/BankOfMaharashtra.py` | `BANK_OF_MAHARASHTRA` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/BOB.py` | `BANK_OF_BARODA` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/BOI.py` | `BANK_OF_INDIA` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Canara.py` | `CANARA` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Central.py` | `CENTRAL_BANK` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Citi.py` | `CITI` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/CityUnion.py` | `CITY_UNION` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/DCB.py` | `DCB` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Federal.py` | `FEDERAL` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/HDFC.py` | `HDFC` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/ICICI.py` | `ICICI` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/IDBI.py` | `IDBI` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/IDFC.py` | `IDFC` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Indian.py` | `INDIAN_BANK` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Indusind.py` | `INDUSIND` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Karnataka.py` | `KARNATAKA` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/KarurVyasa.py` | `KARUR_VYASA` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Kotak.py` | `KOTAK` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Paytm.py` | `PAYTM_BANK` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/PNB.py` | `PNB` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/RBL.py` | `RBL` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Saraswat.py` | `SARASWAT_BANK` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/SBI.py` | `SBI` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/SouthIndian.py` | `SOUTH_INDIAN` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/StandardCharted.py` | `STANDARD_CHARTERED` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/SVC.py` | `SVC_BANK` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Syndicate.py` | `SYNDICATE` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/TamilnadMerchantile.py` | `TAMILNAD_MERCANTILE` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/UCO.py` | `UCO` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/UnionBank.py` | `UNION_BANK` |
| `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/YES.py` | `YES` |

### 3d. The Categorization/Enrichment Pipeline

After the bank-specific script runs, the **Controller** applies a uniform enrichment pipeline for every bank. The pipeline steps, in order:

1. **Bank-level categorization** (`categorize_<BANK>_transactions`) -- Assigns `transactionCategory` (e.g., `ATM_WITHDRAWAL`, `NEFT`, `UPI`, `CHEQUE_PAID`, `CHARGES`, etc.)
2. **Card clean names** (`card_clean_names` in `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/CardCleanNames.py`) -- Only for `CREDIT_CARD` account type. Assigns friendly `partyName` labels.
3. **Card purpose** (`card_purpose` in `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/CardPurpose.py`) -- Only for `CREDIT_CARD`. Assigns `transactionSubcategory` and `merchantType` for credit card transactions.
4. **Purpose** (`purpose` in `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/Purpose.py`) -- For all account types. Assigns `transactionSubcategory` (e.g., `SALARY`, `LOAN_REPAYMENT`, `INSURANCE`, `RENT`, `TRAVEL`, etc.), `merchantType`, and refines `transactionCategory`.
5. **Clean names** (`clean_names` in `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/CleanName.py`) -- For all account types (except credit card). Assigns human-readable `partyName` labels.
6. **Internal payment detection** (`internal_payment` in `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/InternalPayment.py`) -- Only if `accountHolder` is provided. Uses fuzzy matching on the account holder name to detect self-transfers.
7. **Salary detection** (`salary_detection` in `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/SalaryDetection.py`) -- Only for `SAVING` accounts. Uses hierarchical clustering (Levenshtein distance + scipy linkage) to detect salary transactions by recurring credit patterns.
8. **PFM categorization** (`pfm_categorization` in `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/PFMCategorization.py`) -- Only if `isPFMEnabled` is True. Assigns three PFM fields: `pfmCategoryOne` (13 high-level categories: `SALARY`, `INVESTMENT`, `SHOPPING`, `BILL_PAYMENT`, etc.), `pfmCategoryTwo` (37 granular subcategories: `VEHICLE`, `FUEL`, `GROCERIES`, `PHONE_BILL`, etc.), and `pfmCategoryType` (`EXPENSE`, `INCOME`, `TRANSFER`).
9. **Simple description** (`simple_description` in `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/SimpleDescription.py`) -- Only if `isPFMEnabled`. Generates a `pfmSimpleDescription` string.

### 3e. Key Keyword-Related Modules

- **`TransactionCategoryEnums.py`** (`/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/TransactionCategoryEnums.py`, 171 lines) -- Enum of 170+ transaction categories (e.g., `SALARY`, `CHEQUE_PAID`, `CHEQUE_DEPOSIT`, `NEFT`, `IMPS`, `RTGS`, `UPI`, `AUTO_PAYMENT`, `REFUND`, `CHARGES`, etc.). Note the **cheque-related categories**: `CHEQUE_PAID`, `CHEQUE_DEPOSIT`, `CHEQUE_RETURN`, `CHEQUE_RETURN_PENALTY`, `CHEQUE_BOUNCE`, `CHEQUE_BOUNCE_CHARGES`, `CHEQUE_CASH_WITHDRAWAL`, `CHEQUE_RETURN_CHARGES`, `INWARD_CHEQUE_RETURN`, `STOP_CHEQUE_CHARGES`.

- **`TransactionSubcategoryEnums.py`** (`/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/TransactionSubcategoryEnums.py`, 136 lines) -- Enum of 135+ transaction subcategories (e.g., `SALARY`, `LOAN`, `LOAN_REPAYMENT`, `CHEQUE_BOUNCE`, `CHEQUE_BOUNCE_CHARGES`, etc.).

### 3f. Regex Constants (Configuration for Categorization Rules)

- **`RegexConstants.py`** (`/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/RegexConstants.py`, 190 lines) -- The **core configuration file** for categorization rules. Defines ~200 regex string constants used across Purpose, CardPurpose, and CleanName. Includes patterns for: `SALARY`, `LOAN_REP`, `LOAN`, `LOAN_DESC`, `CREDIT_CARD`, `INSURANCE`, `INVEST`, `SHOPPING`, `ONLINE_SHOPPING`, `TRAVEL`, `RENT`, `UTILITY`, `ENTERTAINMENT`, `RESTAU` (restaurants), `FUEL`, `EDU` (education), `MEDICAL`, `COMMUTE`, `ALCOHOL`, `GOV_SER`, `MOBILE_RCH`, `INTERNET`, `DONATION`, and many more. Also defines **merchant type patterns**: `NBFC`, `HFC`, `INSTANT_LOAN`, `FLIGHT`, `TRAIN`, `BUS`, `HOTEL`, `GAMING`, `STREAMING`, `MOVIES`, `GROCERIES`, `PHARMACY`, `HOSPITAL`, etc. There is also a **cheque bounce pattern**: `TECHNICAL_BOUNCE` and `INSUFF_BOUNCE`.

- **`BankRegexConstants.py`** (`/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/BankRegexConstants.py`, 432 lines) -- Bank-specific regex patterns for parsing descriptions, extracting party names, and identifying bank charges, cheque patterns, ECS penalties, reversals, etc. Includes patterns for: SBI, ICICI, HDFC, BOI, Union Bank, PNB, Syndicate, Kotak, Canara, Axis, Indusind, Karur Vyasa, City Union, Bank of Maharashtra, Andhra, UCO, YES, Federal, RBL, Central Bank, DCB, Citi, Paytm, and Standard Chartered. Contains **cheque-specific parsing patterns** (e.g., `HDFC_CHQ_PATTERN1` through `_PATTERN10`, `PNB_CHQ_0` through `_8`, `CHQ_PATTERN1` through `_4`, `FT_CHQ_PATTERN1` through `_37`).

### 3g. Other Utility Scripts

- **`AppUtil.py`** (`/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/AppUtil.py`, 66 lines) -- DataFrame cleaning, string splitting helpers (by `/`, `-`, `~`, `*`, `,`, `:`, `_`, parentheses, brackets), and `fuzzy_match` using `fuzzywuzzy`.

- **`RecurrentTxns.py`** (`/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/RecurrentTxns.py`, 25 lines) -- Uses Levenshtein distance + hierarchical clustering to group transactions by similar party names.

---

## 4. TEST FIXTURE DIRECTORIES

**None found.** There are no `test/`, `tests/`, `fixtures/`, or `data/` directories anywhere in the repository. There are no CSV, JSON, YAML, or other data files that could serve as test fixtures or statement data. The `.gitignore` file explicitly excludes `*.csv`.

---

## 5. CONFIGURATION FILES FOR CATEGORIZATION RULES

There are **no external configuration files** (no YAML, JSON, TOML, INI, or CFG files). All categorization rules are defined as **inline Python code**:

- **Bank-level categorization rules**: Defined inside each `BankScripts/<Bank>.py` as lists of `(regex_condition, category_label)` pairs passed to `np.select()`.
- **Cross-cutting enrichment rules**: Defined in:
  - **`RegexConstants.py`** -- ~200 named regex string constants for subcategory/merchant matching
  - **`BankRegexConstants.py`** -- ~400+ named regex string constants for bank-specific parsing
  - **`Purpose.py`** -- ~150 condition/choice pairs for `transactionSubcategory`, ~15 for `transactionCategory`, ~95 for `merchantType`
  - **`CardPurpose.py`** -- ~100 condition/choice pairs for credit card `transactionSubcategory`
  - **`CleanName.py`** -- ~500+ condition/choice pairs for `partyName` assignment
  - **`CardCleanNames.py`** -- ~900+ lines of condition/choice pairs for credit card `partyName` assignment
  - **`InternalPayment.py`** -- Fuzzy name matching to detect self-transfers
  - **`PFMCategorization.py`** -- ~80 condition/choice pairs for PFM category one, ~105 for PFM category two, ~20 for PFM category type
  - **`SalaryDetection.py`** -- Algorithmic (not rule-based) salary detection using hierarchical clustering
</task_result>
</task>

### Common Pattern Summary Across All Four Banks

Every bank script follows the same pattern:
- **CHEQUE_PAID** = `DEBIT` transactions matching `CLG`, `CHQ`, `CHEQUE`, or `CHEQ NO` keywords
- **CHEQUE_DEPOSIT** = `CREDIT` transactions matching the same clearing/cheque keywords
- **CHEQUE_RETURN** = return/reject patterns on both CREDIT (inward return) and DEBIT (outward return)
- **CHEQUE_CASH_WITHDRAWAL** = "CASH CHEQUE", "BRN-TO CASH", or remark-number-based cash withdrawals
- **CHEQUE_RETURN_PENALTY** = penalty/charge descriptions containing `chq.*return.*charg` etc.
- **STOP_CHEQUE_CHARGES** = "CHQ STOP" pattern on DEBIT
- All banks default to **"TRANSFER"** for unmatched transactions

## 3. Purpose.py Lines 150-170 -- transactionCategory Refinement

### `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/Purpose.py`, lines 154-170

This is the **second block of `conditions_choices`** in the file, where it **re-assigns `transactionCategory`** after the first block handled `transactionSubcategory`:

```python
conditions_choices = [
    [transactions.description.str.contains(TDS, na=False, case=False) & transactions.type.eq("DEBIT") , "TDS"],
    [transactions.description.str.contains(TAX_REFUND, na=False, case=False)  ,  "TAX_REFUND"],
    [transactions.description.str.contains(CASHBACK, na=False, case=False)    ,  "CASHBACK"],
    [transactions.description.str.contains(REFUND, na=False, case=False)      ,  "REFUND"],
    [transactions.description.str.contains(DIRECT_TAX, na=False, case=False)  ,  "DIRECT_TAX"],
    [transactions.description.str.contains(INDIRECT_TAX, na=False, case=False),  "INDIRECT_TAX"],
    [transactions.description.str.contains(TAX, na=False, case=False) , "TAX"],
    [transactions.description.str.contains(FD, na=False, case=False) & transactions.type.eq("DEBIT") , "FIXED_DEPOSIT"],
    [transactions.description.str.contains(INTEREST, na=False, case=False) & transactions.type.eq("DEBIT") , "INTEREST_PAID"],
    [transactions.description.str.contains(INTEREST, na=False, case=False) & transactions.type.eq("CREDIT") , "INTEREST"],
    [transactions.transactionSubcategory.eq("LOAN") & transactions.amount.isin([1,-1]) , "ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION"],   # <-- line 165
    [transactions.transactionSubcategory.eq("POS_RENTAL"), "TRANSFER"],                                            # <-- line 166
]

df = pd.DataFrame(data=conditions_choices, columns=['conditions', 'choices'])
transactions['transactionCategory'] = np.select(df['conditions'], df['choices'], default=transactions.transactionCategory)
```

**Line 165**: When `transactionSubcategory` is "LOAN" **and** the amount is exactly 1 or -1, it reclassifies the `transactionCategory` from whatever it was to `"ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION"`. This catches penny-drop / micro-deposit verification transactions that were initially misclassified as loans.

**Line 166**: When `transactionSubcategory` is "POS_RENTAL", it reclassifies the `transactionCategory` to `"TRANSFER"`. POS rental (EDC/MMC rental) transactions are treated as regular transfers rather than POS transactions.

The flow is identical across all bank scripts. In every `BankScripts/{BANK}.py` file, the function `categorize_{BANK}_transactions(transactions, accountType)` follows this pattern:

# 5. AppUtil.py -- Complete Utility Functions

**File:** `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/AppUtil.py` (66 lines)

```python
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import re
from fuzzywuzzy import fuzz

def clean_input_df(transactions):
    """Strips whitespace from description, converts amount and balanceAfterTransaction to numeric."""
    transactions['description'] = transactions['description'].str.strip()
    transactions['amount'] = pd.to_numeric(transactions['amount'])
    transactions['balanceAfterTransaction'] = pd.to_numeric(transactions['balanceAfterTransaction'])
    return transactions

# --- String splitting utilities ---
# Each splits a pandas Series of strings by a delimiter and joins a slice back.
def sep_by_(description, n1, n2):
    return description.str.split('/').str[n1:n2].str.join('')

def sep_by_dash(description, n1, n2):
    return description.str.split('-').str[n1:n2].str.join('')

def sep_by_wave(description, n1, n2):
    return description.str.split('~').str[n1:n2].str.join('')

def sep_by_star(description, n1, n2):
    return description.str.split('*').str[n1:n2].str.join('')

def sep_by_comma(description, n1, n2):
    return description.str.split(',').str[n1:n2].str.join('')

def sep_by_colon(description, n1, n2):
    return description.str.split(':').str[n1:n2].str.join('')

def sep_by_under(description, n1, n2):
    return description.str.split('_').str[n1:n2].str.join('')

def sep_by_brckt(description, n1, n2):
    return description.str.split('(').str[n1:n2].str.join('')

def sep_by_right_brckt(description, n1, n2):
    return description.str.split(')').str[n1:n2].str.join('')

def sep_by_square_brckt(description, n1, n2):
    return description.str.split('[').str[n1:n2].str.join('')

def sep_by_square_right_brckt(description, n1, n2):
    return description.str.split(']').str[n1:n2].str.join('')

# --- Fuzzy matching ---
def fuzzy_match(s1, s2, dist):
    """Returns True if fuzzywuzzy ratio between s1 and s2 exceeds dist."""
    if fuzz.ratio(s1, s2) > dist:
        return True
    return False

# --- Core regex extraction ---
def py_extract(description, pat, index):
    """
    Custom implementation to extract a string from a pandas Series
    using a regex pattern, with null/NaN/None handling.
    @params description: input pandas Series
    @params pat: regular expression
    @params index: group index to extract
    """
    res = description.str.extract(pat=pat, flags=re.IGNORECASE).get(index)
    if res is None:
        return pd.Series("")
    res.fillna("", inplace=True)
    return res
```

---

## Summary of the partyName Extraction Architecture

1. **Initialize** `transactionCategory` via bank-specific categorization patterns
2. **Initialize** `partyName = ""`
3. **Extract** raw partyName from `description` using bank-specific regex/string-splitting rules (sep_by_dash, sep_by_, py_extract, etc.)
4. **Refine** partyName via secondary np.select pass (remove boilerplate like "TP ACH", "BD-", dates, bank codes)
5. **Clean** with a tertiary pass (remove "Micr Clg", "Chq Paid", single letters, etc.)
6. **Create** `party2` = partyName with spaces removed
7. **Call** `clean_names()` from `CleanName.py` to standardize names across banks (merge known entities, resolve aliases, etc.)

All utility functions for splitting (`sep_by_*`) and regex extraction (`py_extract`) are defined in `AppUtil.py`.

For F-5, the issue is that account-verification transactions (penny-drop deposits) are not being caught. Let me trace through the code:

In Purpose.py line 165:
```python
[transactions.transactionSubcategory.eq("LOAN") & transactions.amount.isin([1,-1]) , "ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION"],
```

This checks if the `transactionSubcategory` is "LOAN" AND the amount is 1 or -1. But the transactionSubcategory is set in the first block of Purpose.py (lines 12-149). The "LOAN" subcategory is set at lines 23-25:
```python
[transactions.partyName.str.contains(LOAN, na=False, case=False) & ... & transactions.type.eq("CREDIT") & ... & transactions.amount.gt(200), "LOAN"],
[transactions.party2.str.contains(LOAN, ...) & ... & transactions.type.eq("CREDIT") & ... & transactions.amount.gt(200), "LOAN"],
[transactions.description.str.contains(LOAN_DESC, ...) & transactions.type.eq("CREDIT") & ... & transactions.amount.gt(200), "LOAN"],
```

These all require `amount.gt(200)`! So a transaction with amount 1 or -1 would never match the "LOAN" subcategory because of the amount > 200 condition.

Wait, but the bank-level ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION detection should catch this. Let me re-check HDFC.py line 13:
```python
[transactions.description.str.contains(BANK_VER, na=False, case=False) & transactions.amount.lt(2) , "ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION"],
```

This checks if the description contains BANK_VER patterns AND the amount is less than 2. BANK_VER is defined in BankRegexConstants.py. Let me check what BANK_VER contains.

Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me now systematically analyze each finding and identify the root cause.

**F-2: "CHQ PAID-MICR CLG-RAVI KUMAR" → counterparty shows internal clearing tag**

I traced through HDFC.py and found that line 163 matches the "CHQ PAID-" pattern first and extracts an empty string using `sep_by_dash(desc, 3, 4)`, which overrides the correct cheque pattern at line 190. The fix is to move the cheque patterns before line 163 or modify line 163's condition.

**F-5: Token account-verification deposit → TRANSFER**

Looking at Purpose.py line 165: `[transactions.transactionSubcategory.eq("LOAN") & transactions.amount.isin([1,-1]), "ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION"]`

The issue is that the `transactionSubcategory` is only set to "LOAN" when the amount is > 200 (lines 23-25). So a transaction with amount 1 or -1 would never have the subcategory "LOAN", and the ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION reclassification would never trigger.

But wait, the bank scripts have their own ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION detection. For example:
- HDFC.py line 13: `[transactions.description.str.contains(BANK_VER, ...) & transactions.amount.lt(2), "ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION"]`
- SBI.py line 12: `[transactions.description.str.contains(ACC_VERIF, ...) & transactions.type.eq("CREDIT") & transactions.amount.eq(1), "ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION"]`

So the bank-level detection should catch these. But the finding says the token deposit is being filed as TRANSFER. This means the description doesn't match the BANK_VER/ACC_VERIF patterns for the specific bank.

The Purpose.py fallback at line 165 is supposed to catch any leftover penny-drop transactions by checking if the subcategory is "LOAN" and the amount is 1 or -1. But it can't work because the subcategory is never set to "LOAN" for amount < 200.

The fix for F-5 should be to make the account-verification detection in Purpose.py more robust. Instead of requiring `transactionSubcategory.eq("LOAN")`, it should also check for other conditions.

All require amount > 200. So subcategory "LOAN" can never be set for amount 1 or -1. The line 165 is effectively dead code.

The issue is that the bank-level categorization might not catch the penny-drop either. Let me look at the bank-level ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION patterns more carefully.

The BANK_VER pattern (used by HDFC, Axis, Indusind, BOM):
```
PennyD rop|Accou|Test Pa|AccVeri|untVe|valid|uNT VE|Authentica|verif|pennydrop|valida|GOOGLE TEM|GOOGLE.*SERVICE|bajaj|Re\\s?mitter |BENE NAME|ntVer|ary Ver|ils va|TEMPORA|ny\\s?Test|ny\\s?drop
```

This pattern includes "verif", "valida", "Accou", "valid", "Authentica", "Test Pa", "AccVeri", etc. These are very broad and would match many common descriptions. But the issue might be that the specific token deposit description doesn't match any of these patterns.

For the SBI bank, ACC_VERIF is:
```
Account va|Bank det|AccVerify|Bank\\s?_?Acc|pennyd|Account v|accv|Acc ver|Bank det|bank ver|[0-9]/BV[0-9]|ver|/bene|/bav|BANK DETAILS VALID
```

This includes "ver" which is very broad and would match many descriptions. But again, the specific token deposit might not match.

For PNB, ACC_VERIFY_PNB is:
```
A/C VALIDATION|BE NE NAME|ACC VALI
```

This is more specific and might not match some token deposits.

steps.message: Now I have a thorough understanding. Let me implement the fixes for the findings I can clearly identify
steps.reasoning_content:
Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me analyze each finding and determine the fix:

**F-1: Bare-number cheque debit → TRANSFER**
Looking at the bank scripts, several banks have remark-based cheque detection:
- PNB.py line 60: `[transactions.remark.str.contains("^[0-9]{5}$", ...) & transactions.type.eq("DEBIT"), "CHEQUE_PAID"]`
- Kotak.py line 241: `[transactions.transactionCategory.eq("CHEQUE_PAID") & transactions.remark.str.contains("^[0-9]{3,5}$", ...), transactions.description]`

But this is only for partyName extraction, not categorization. For categorization, most banks rely on description keywords (CHQ, CHEQUE, CLG, etc.).

The finding says "a short, bare run of digits with no other text sitting beside it." This could be in the `description` or `remark` field. Without knowing which bank, I need to look for a common pattern.

Actually, looking at the finding more carefully: "The miss is confined to this one bare-number note form." This suggests it's a specific bank's format. Let me look at which banks might have bare-number cheque descriptions.

Looking at the PNB.py categorization:
- Line 60: `[transactions.remark.str.contains("^[0-9]{5}$", ...) & transactions.type.eq("DEBIT"), "CHEQUE_PAID"]`
- Line 61: `[transactions.remark.str.contains("^[0-9]{5,6}$", ...) & transactions.type.eq("CREDIT"), "CHEQUE_DEPOSIT"]`

PNB already has a rule for 5-digit bare numbers in remark. But what about 6-digit numbers? Line 61 covers 5-6 digit numbers for CREDIT, but line 60 only covers 5-digit numbers for DEBIT. So a 6-digit number in the remark for a DEBIT transaction would fall through.

Wait, actually, line 61 says `^[0-9]{5,6}$` for CREDIT, and line 60 says `^[0-9]{5}$` for DEBIT. So a 6-digit number in remark for a DEBIT would not be caught.

steps.message:
Here is a summary of all changes made:

### Files modified (5 changes across 4 files):

**1. `UtilityScripts/Purpose.py` — F-5 fix**
- Line 165: Changed the account-verification fallback from requiring `transactionSubcategory.eq("LOAN")` (which was unreachable for amounts <200) to directly checking `amount.isin([1,-1])` against generic transfer categories (`TRANSFER`, `NEFT`, `IMPS`, `RTGS`, `UPI`, `FUND_TRANSFER`).

**2. `BankScripts/PNB.py` — F-1 fix**
- Line 60: Extended remark-based cheque detection for DEBIT from `^[0-9]{5}$` to `^[0-9]{5,6}$`, matching the CREDIT pattern at line 61.

**3. `BankScripts/HDFC.py` — F-2, F-3, F-4 fix**
- Moved cheque-specific name extraction patterns (lines 187-197 originally) to before the `"CHQ PAID-"` generic pattern (line 163 originally) to prevent the wrong extraction from overriding correct cheque name resolution.
- Added exclusion of `"MICR CLG"` from the `"CHQ PAID-"` condition to prevent it from matching cheques that the dedicated cheque patterns should handle.
- Added a 4-part NEFT/RTGS/IMPS fallback (3 dashes) extracting index 3 instead of index 2, placed before the general 3-part fallback.
- Changed `HDFC_CHQ_PATTERN3` extraction from `sep_by_colon(sep_by_dash(...), 1, 2)` → `sep_by_dash(...)` to handle names without trailing colons.

**4. `BankScripts/SBI.py` — F-3, F-4 fix**
- Added a NEFT fallback for 4-part `*`-separated descriptions (3 `*` chars) extracting index 2 instead of index 3.
- Added an IMPS fallback for 4-part `/`-separated descriptions (3 `/` chars) extracting index 2 instead of index 3.

**5. `UtilityScripts/BankRegexConstants.py` — F-2 fix**
- `HDFC_CHQ_PATTERN3`: Changed from `"MICR CLG - [A-Z]+"` to `"MICR CLG ?- ?[A-Z]+"` to match descriptions where the dash is directly attached without spaces (e.g., `CHQ PAID-MICR CLG-RAVI KUMAR`).
